title: aquilegia canadensis subsp. flavescens
intro: Aquilegia canadensis subsp. flavescens, also known as yellow columbine, is a species of flowering plant in the buttercup family that is native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of yellow columbine include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce menstrual cramps. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in poultices and salves for topical application.
